---
title: "GP3: Product Feed and Retail Media Setup"
author: "Brian V, Ron S, Name 3, Name 4, Name 5"
date: today
format: 
  html: 
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    number-sections: true
    code-link: true
    embed-resources: true
editor: visual
execute: 
  freeze: auto
---

## Key Question

**How can CPP Farm Store prepare its product information for Google Merchant Center, Amazon Ads, or another retail-media channel?**

## Step-by-Step Guidance

### Step 1: Choose a retail-media or product-listing channel

Your group should focus on at least one channel. Recommended options: \* Google Merchant Center \* Google Shopping / Shopping Ads \* Amazon listing logic \* Amazon Ads concept \* Another approved retail-media platform

> **Note:** Google Merchant Center is recommended because it connects clearly to product feeds, product listings, search visibility, and ecommerce traffic.

### Step 2: Select products

Choose a focused set of products from your Shopify mini-store. Suggested number: \* **Minimum:** 5 products \* **Recommended:** 8–12 products

*Choose products that make sense for online discovery or promotion.*

### Step 3: Create product feed fields

Your product feed should include relevant fields such as: \* Product ID \* Product title \* Product description \* Price \* Availability \* Product category \* Product type \* Brand \* Condition \* Image link or image recommendation \* Landing page URL \* Shipping or pickup information \* Promotion message \* Custom label, if useful

### Step 4: Improve product titles

Product titles should be clear, searchable, and customer-friendly. \* **Weak title example:** Jam \* **Better title example:** CPP Farm Store Strawberry Jam, 12 oz

Good product titles often include: \* Brand or store name \* Product type \* Key attribute \* Size, flavor, or quantity \* Seasonal or local relevance, if appropriate

### Step 5: Improve product descriptions (Brian)

Descriptions should help customers understand value. Strong descriptions may include: \* What the product is \* Key benefits or features \* Suggested use \* Local or seasonal relevance \* Pickup or availability note \* Gift or bundle potential

**1. CPP Farm Store Local Gift Basket**
* **What the product is:** A carefully curated collection of premium CPP Farm Store favorites, beautifully packaged and ready to give.
* **Key benefits or features:** Takes the guesswork out of gifting by offering a high-quality, pre-arranged selection of farm-fresh and artisanal goods.
* **Suggested use:** Perfect for corporate gifts, holiday presents, or a thoughtful "thank you" for friends, family, and professors.
* **Local or seasonal relevance:** Features seasonal jams, snacks, and treats crafted by Cal Poly Pomona students and local artisans.
* **Gift or bundle potential:** Acts as a complete gift bundle in itself, but pairs wonderfully with a CPP greeting card or a fresh campus floral arrangement.
* **Pickup or availability note:** Available for convenient in-store pickup (pre-order recommended during busy holiday seasons).
* **Final Feed Description:** 
> "Share the best of the farm with the CPP Farm Store Local Gift Basket. This beautifully packaged collection features premium, seasonal treats and jams crafted by Cal Poly Pomona students and local artisans. Perfect for holiday presents, corporate gifting, or a thoughtful 'thank you,' this curated box takes the guesswork out of giving. Pair it with a local greeting card for the ultimate farm-to-table gift. Available now for in-store pickup at the Cal Poly Pomona Farm Store (pre-ordering is recommended during the holidays)."

**2. CPP Campus Apiary Raw Honey (16 oz)**
* **What the product is:** Pure, raw, and unfiltered local honey in a classic 16 oz glass jar.
* **Key benefits or features:** Offers a rich, natural sweetness and preserves the natural pollen and enzymes missing from commercial honeys.
* **Suggested use:** Ideal for sweetening hot tea, drizzling over morning yogurt and granola, or elevating your baking recipes.
* **Local or seasonal relevance:** Harvested directly from the Cal Poly Pomona campus apiary, offering a true taste of the local flora.
* **Gift or bundle potential:** An excellent addition to any gift basket; pairs perfectly with our farm-fresh fruit or herbal tea selections.
* **Pickup or availability note:** Available for quick grab-and-go in-store pickup.
* **Final Feed Description:** 
> "Taste the local landscape with our CPP Campus Apiary Raw Honey (16 oz). Harvested directly from the Cal Poly Pomona campus apiary, this pure, unfiltered honey preserves the natural pollen and rich flavors you won't find in commercial alternatives. It is the perfect natural sweetener for hot teas, drizzling over yogurt, or elevating your favorite baking recipes. Bundle it with our herbal teas or fresh fruit for a sweet local gift. Available today for grab-and-go in-store pickup."

**3. CPP Seasonal Fresh Produce Box**
* **What the product is:** A mixed, pre-packed box of seasonal, freshly harvested vegetables and fruits.
* **Key benefits or features:** Provides convenient variety, ultimate farm-to-table freshness, and supports sustainable campus agriculture.
* **Suggested use:** Great for weekly meal prepping, fresh juicing, or inspiring new seasonal culinary creations at home.
* **Local or seasonal relevance:** Contents rotate weekly to feature exactly what is currently in season and growing on the CPP campus farm.
* **Gift or bundle potential:** Makes a fantastic, healthy care package for students, or can be bundled with a jar of our campus honey.
* **Pickup or availability note:** Order online for scheduled weekly in-store pickup.
* **Final Feed Description:** 
> "Bring the harvest directly to your kitchen with the CPP Seasonal Fresh Produce Box. This mixed box is packed with the freshest vegetables and fruits, rotating weekly to feature exactly what is currently in season on the Cal Poly Pomona campus farm. Supporting sustainable agriculture, this box offers incredible farm-to-table freshness that is perfect for weekly meal prepping, juicing, or healthy snacking. It also makes a wonderful, healthy care package for local students! Order online today for your scheduled weekly in-store pickup."

### Step 6: Check image readiness

Product images matter for ecommerce and retail media. Identify whether each product has: \* Clear image \* Accurate representation \* Good lighting \* Simple background \* Appropriate size or resolution \* No confusing text overlays \* Consistent style across products

*If images are not available, describe what image would be needed.*

### Step 7: Identify feed-quality concerns

For each product, identify potential problems such as: \* Missing product image \* Incomplete description \* Unclear price \* Unclear availability \* No landing page \* Weak product title \* Product category uncertainty \* Missing brand information \* Shipping or pickup ambiguity

### Step 8: Explain retail-media strategy

This product feed supports the campaign plan by giving each product a distinct role rather than promoting all three equally.

The **CPP Campus Apiary Raw Honey** should be promoted first. It's low-cost, grab-and-go, and has the clearest local story of the three — making it the easiest entry point for someone discovering the store through Google Shopping for the first time, and the best product for building initial search relevance before layering in the others.

The **CPP Seasonal Fresh Produce Box** is best suited for seasonal and recurring campaigns, since its entire appeal is built around what's currently in season on campus. This makes it a natural fit for retargeting ads and ongoing email/social content that gives customers a reason to keep coming back week after week, rather than a one-time purchase.

The **CPP Farm Store Local Gift Basket** is the clearest fit for gift buyers, with the highest price point of the three and a pre-curated format that removes decision fatigue. This is the product to prioritize around key gifting windows — finals week, graduation, and holidays — where a pre-order/pickup model helps address the fulfillment friction our group identified earlier.

Together, the three products cover three different campaign goals — acquisition (Honey), retention (Produce Box), and high-value occasions (Gift Basket) — rather than competing for the same customer at the same time.
